article_title: NASA Switches Off Voyager 1's Low-Energy Charged Particles Experiment After Nearly 49 Years to Buy Time for the 'Big Bang' Power Fix

content_preview.title: NASA Switches Off Voyager 1's Low-Energy Charged Particles Experiment After Nearly 49 Years to Buy Time for the 'Big Bang' Power Fix
content_preview.summary: NASA powered down Voyager 1's LECP instrument on April 17, 2026 to extend the interstellar probe's life by about a year while engineers prepare a more ambitious power-saving overhaul.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

NASA engineers at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California sent commands on April 17, 2026 to shut down the Low-energy Charged Particles experiment (LECP) aboard Voyager 1, the most distant human-made object in space. The instrument had been operating almost without interruption since the spacecraft's 1977 launch — nearly 49 years of continuous data collection — and its retirement is part of a deliberate power-conservation strategy intended to keep the interstellar probe...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Space News piece. Tone neutral, structure clear (Overview / What We Know / Big Bang Plan / What We Don't Know / Context).
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 4 snapshots. NASA blog verifies April 17 commands + 7-of-10 instruments shut off. JPL verifies LECP measuring ions/electrons/cosmic rays from solar system + galaxy. Space.com verifies Voyager 2 LECP March 2025 shutdown + 'engineers agreed years ago on shutdown order'. Universe Today verifies LECP pressure-fronts/density measurements, RTG ~4W/year decline + less-than-half-launch-power, 2 remaining instruments (plasma waves + magnetic fields), Badaruddin quote verbatim, 'Big Bang' plan structure (simultaneous power down + low-power alternatives), May-June 2026 V2 tests + V1 no sooner than July, LECP potential reactivation. Minor wording: Space.com says '24 billion kilometers' while article uses '25 billion km' (the 25 figure is verbatim in NASA blog and Universe Today, so the article's number is correct but the parenthetical attribution chain is mixed). '2012 interstellar crossing' is inferred from Universe Today's 'more than six years before November 2018 [Voyager 2]' — well-known public fact, defensible.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All material claims verify. Two minor info-level nits (Space.com km figure differs by 1B; '2012' inferred not verbatim). Otherwise rigorous.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. Distinct from yesterday's Mars + space coverage.
recommendations: Optional: tighten the km parenthetical to attribute to NASA blog (which uses 25 billion km) rather than Space.com (which uses 24).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE. Excellent deep-space-probe piece, every material claim sourced. Ready to merge.
